summary: Iteration 38 — Driver Onboarding (Individual + Fleet) verified end-to-end. Backend pytest suite (5/5 passing): individual register OK, fleet register without company_name → 400 (both missing and whitespace-only), fleet register success creates Company + sets company_id on driver, admin /api/admin/fleet/companies lists the new company with correct business_id and owner_email. Frontend (Expo web, 390x844) — Individual flow correctly shows 'Step 1..5 of 5' across account→name→contact→vehicle→review, registration POST returns 200. Fleet flow correctly shows 'Step 1..6 of 6', the Company step appears at Step 4 of 6, Continue is disabled (aria-disabled=true) when company name is empty (validation works), Review shows Account=Fleet/Company and the company name, registration POST returns 200 and an active company is created. NOTE: On the web preview, react-native-web's Alert.alert does NOT render the success dialog button, so the post-registration 'Continue to KYC' callback (router.replace('/kyc')) never fires — user stays on the Review screen even though registration succeeded. Same limitation makes tooltip alerts (tooltip-companyName/businessId/licenseClass) invisible on web. Both are web-only RNW Alert limitations and would work on native iOS/Android.

ui_bugs.issue: On web, react-native-web does not invoke Alert.alert button callbacks, so router.replace('/kyc') never runs after successful registration. User remains on Review screen. Backend record is created correctly. Works on native but a real UX regression on web preview. Consider Platform.OS === 'web' branch that calls router.replace('/kyc') directly (with a toast) instead of relying on the Alert button. Reference: /app/frontend/app/onboarding.tsx:226-233

Tooltips (tooltip-companyName, tooltip-businessId, tooltip-licenseClass, tooltip-capacity, tooltip-plate) use Alert.alert which is silently dropped by react-native-web. The information icon is tappable but no helper text appears on web. Will work on native. Consider a small in-app popover/Modal for web parity. Reference: /app/frontend/app/onboarding.tsx:156-169

action_items: Optional UX polish: branch on Platform.OS === 'web' in handleSubmit to navigate to /kyc directly (since Alert.alert button callbacks don't fire on react-native-web).
Optional UX polish: replace Tooltip Alert.alert with an in-app Modal/Popover so tooltips also work on web preview.
No code changes required for the task itself — Individual (5-step) and Fleet (6-step) flows, validation gate, and backend side effects all behave as specified.
critical_code_review_comments: onboarding.tsx is 1027 lines — consider splitting steps into per-step components for maintainability.
handleSubmit relies on Alert.alert's onPress callback for routing; that callback is not invoked on react-native-web. Either branch by Platform or replace with an inline success state + auto-redirect.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (flows + validation); Alert-based post-flow UX limited on web only

seed_data_creation: Created 2 throw-away drivers (qa.ind+<ts>@example.com, qa.fleet+<ts>@example.com) and 1 company 'QA Fleet Oy <ts>' via the public /api/driver/register endpoint, plus pytest also created additional disposable users (TEST_-pattern not enforced because endpoint hashes the email as-is). All use unique timestamps so no collisions.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 38 onboarding wizard is functional end-to-end. Use /app/backend/tests/test_iter38_onboarding_fleet.py for regression. Only known limitation: post-registration Alert.alert callback (router.replace('/kyc')) and Tooltip Alerts don't fire on react-native-web — backend registration still succeeds. Test credentials: admin@nadaruns.com / admin123.
rca_of_issue: Why user remains on Review on web after Create Account: react-native-web's Alert.alert ignores the buttons array's onPress callbacks (it maps to window.alert without callback). Since router.replace('/kyc') is wrapped inside that callback, it never executes on web. Native iOS/Android implementations of Alert do fire the callback, so this is a web-only manifestation.
